name: un_acked_always_empty
code:
proof fn phi_1_un_acked_always_empty(sd: CSingleDelivery, src: EndPoint, dst: EndPoint)
    requires
        sd.valid(),
        src.abstractable(),
        sd.send_state@.contains_key(dst@),
    ensures
        sd@.un_acked_messages_for_dest(src@, dst@) =~= Set::<Packet>::empty(),
{
}
reason: If provable, retransmit_un_acked_packets_for_dst never adds any packets, making retransmission a no-op

name: zero_count_nonempty
code:
proof fn phi_2_zero_count_nonempty(sd: CSingleDelivery, src: EndPoint, dst: EndPoint)
    requires
        sd.valid(),
        src.abstractable(),
        sd.send_state@.contains_key(dst@),
    ensures
        exists |p: Packet| sd@.un_acked_messages_for_dest_up_to(src@, dst@, 0 as nat).contains(p),
{
}
reason: If provable, the zero-count prefix set is non-empty, indicating an off-by-one error in the set comprehension bound

name: routing_src_equals_dst
code:
proof fn phi_3_routing_src_equals_dst(sd: CSingleDelivery, src: EndPoint, dst: EndPoint, p: Packet)
    requires
        sd.valid(),
        src.abstractable(),
        sd.send_state@.contains_key(dst@),
        sd@.un_acked_messages_for_dest(src@, dst@).contains(p),
    ensures
        p.src == p.dst,
{
}
reason: If provable, every retransmitted packet is sent back to its own source, indicating a routing confusion between src and dst

name: un_acked_at_most_singleton
code:
proof fn phi_4_un_acked_at_most_singleton(sd: CSingleDelivery, src: EndPoint, dst: EndPoint, p1: Packet, p2: Packet)
    requires
        sd.valid(),
        src.abstractable(),
        sd.send_state@.contains_key(dst@),
        sd@.un_acked_messages_for_dest(src@, dst@).contains(p1),
        sd@.un_acked_messages_for_dest(src@, dst@).contains(p2),
    ensures
        p1 == p2,
{
}
reason: If provable, the un-acked set never contains more than one distinct packet, meaning batched retransmission is impossible

name: count_increment_no_growth
code:
proof fn phi_5_count_increment_no_growth(sd: CSingleDelivery, src: EndPoint, dst: EndPoint, n: nat)
    requires
        sd.valid(),
        src.abstractable(),
        sd.send_state@.contains_key(dst@),
        n < sd@.send_state[dst@].un_acked.len(),
    ensures
        sd@.un_acked_messages_for_dest_up_to(src@, dst@, n + 1) =~= sd@.un_acked_messages_for_dest_up_to(src@, dst@, n),
{
}
reason: If provable, adding the next un-acked message to the prefix never enlarges the set, so accumulation of retransmit obligations is broken
